SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.valve

Class ControlValveGasSizing_IEC_60534_2_1

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.valve.ControlValveGasSizing_IEC_60534_2_1
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ControlValveGasSizing_IEC_60534_2_1 extends Object implements Serializable
Compressible (gas/vapour) control-valve sizing per IEC 60534-2-1.

Computes the required flow coefficient (Kv and Cv) for a control valve in compressible service, including:

  • pressure-drop ratio x = Δp / p1;
  • specific-heat-ratio factor Fγ = γ / 1.40;
  • choked-flow detection from the terminal pressure-drop ratio factor xT;
  • expansion factor Y = 1 - x / (3 · Fγ · xT), bounded to 2/3;
  • piping geometry factor Fp.

The mass-flow sizing equation is W = N6 · Fp · C · Y · sqrt(x · p1 · ρ1), solved for the required flow coefficient C (= Kv). With W in kg/h, p1 in bar (absolute) and ρ1 in kg/m3, the numerical constant N6 = 27.3.

This is a standalone sizing calculator and is a companion to ControlValveNoise_IEC_60534_8_3. It does not modify a process valve; the resulting Kv/Cv can be applied separately via ThrottlingValve.setCv().

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

  • Constructor Details

    • ControlValveGasSizing_IEC_60534_2_1

      public ControlValveGasSizing_IEC_60534_2_1()
      Default constructor for ControlValveGasSizing_IEC_60534_2_1.

  • Method Details

    • setFlowConditions

      public void setFlowConditions(double massFlowKgH, double inletPressureBara, double outletPressureBara, double inletDensityKgM3)
      Sets the flow and pressure conditions.
      Parameters:
      massFlowKgH - mass flow rate in kg/h (must be > 0)
      inletPressureBara - inlet absolute pressure in bar (must be > 0)
      outletPressureBara - outlet absolute pressure in bar (must be > 0 and < inlet)
      inletDensityKgM3 - inlet density in kg/m3 (must be > 0)

    • setValveCoefficients

      public void setValveCoefficients(double isentropicExp, double xt, double fp)
      Sets the gas property and valve coefficients.
      Parameters:
      isentropicExp - isentropic exponent gamma = Cp/Cv (must be > 1)
      xt - terminal pressure-drop ratio factor xT (0-1, must be > 0)
      fp - piping geometry factor Fp (must be > 0)

    • fromValve

      public void fromValve(ThrottlingValve valve)
      Populates the flow conditions directly from a NeqSim process ThrottlingValve.

      Reads the mass flow, density and isentropic exponent from the valve inlet fluid and the inlet/outlet pressures from the valve streams (the valve must already have been run/flashed). The valve coefficients xT and Fp are left unchanged so they can be configured separately via setValveCoefficients(double, double, double).

      Parameters:
      valve - the process throttling valve supplying the flow conditions (must not be null and must have inlet and outlet streams)

    • calcSizing

      public void calcSizing()
      Runs the IEC 60534-2-1 compressible sizing calculation.

    • getPressureDropRatio

      public double getPressureDropRatio()
      Returns the pressure-drop ratio x = dp / p1.
      Returns:
      pressure-drop ratio (dimensionless)

    • getSpecificHeatRatioFactor

      public double getSpecificHeatRatioFactor()
      Returns the specific-heat-ratio factor Fgamma = gamma / 1.40.
      Returns:
      specific-heat-ratio factor (dimensionless)

    • getChokedPressureDropRatio

      public double getChokedPressureDropRatio()
      Returns the choked pressure-drop ratio Fgamma * xT.
      Returns:
      choked pressure-drop ratio (dimensionless)

    • isChoked

      public boolean isChoked()
      Returns whether the flow is choked.
      Returns:
      true when the flow is choked

    • getExpansionFactor

      public double getExpansionFactor()
      Returns the expansion factor Y.
      Returns:
      expansion factor (dimensionless)

    • getRequiredKv

      public double getRequiredKv()
      Returns the required flow coefficient Kv.
      Returns:
      required Kv (m3/h at unit pressure drop)

    • getRequiredCv

      public double getRequiredCv()
      Returns the required flow coefficient Cv.
      Returns:
      required Cv (US gpm at unit pressure drop)

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serializes the calculation results to a pretty-printed JSON string.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of the results
